title: Building the Colosseum: Rome's Greatest Stage

scenes.visual_description: Aerial reveal of ancient Rome circa 70 CE; the Colosseum site is a marshy lake fed by Nero's Domus Aurea fountains.
scenes.narration: Before it became Rome's greatest stage, this site was a private lake at the heart of an emperor's palace.

scenes.visual_description: Vespasian's engineers drain the lake; foundation trenches are excavated and concrete piers are poured.
scenes.narration: Vespasian's engineers drained the lake and laid 12-meter-deep concrete foundations — a feat unmatched in the ancient world.

scenes.visual_description: Travertine blocks rise tier by tier; iron clamps and wooden cranes lift stone into place.
scenes.camera_motion: vertical dolly tracking the rising structure
scenes.narration: Each tier required 100,000 cubic meters of travertine, hauled 30 kilometers from quarries at Tibur.

scenes.visual_description: Cutaway of the hypogeum: trap doors, animal lifts, and the wooden arena floor.
scenes.narration: Beneath the sand floor, an ingenious wooden machine raised animals, scenery, and gladiators on cue.

scenes.visual_description: Inaugural games of 80 CE: the velarium awning unfurls, 50,000 spectators take their seats.
scenes.camera_motion: wide pull-back from arena floor to stadium-wide hero shot
scenes.narration: When the Colosseum opened in 80 CE, Titus celebrated with one hundred days of games.

scenes.visual_description: Time-lapse from completion through medieval reuse to modern conservation.
scenes.narration: Two thousand years later, the Colosseum still stands — partly ruin, partly machine, entirely Rome.
